classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Council in Trullo; Often Called The Quinisext Council

title: Canon LXXVII.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: It is not right that those who are dedicated to religion, whether clerics or ascetics, should wash in the bath with women, nor should any Christian man or layman do so.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: For this is severely condemned by the heathens.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: But if any one is caught in this thing, if he is a cleric let him be deposed; if a layman, let him be cut off.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: A Christian man shall not bathe with women.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: Should a cleric do so he is to be deposed, and a layman cut off.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: This is a renewal of the XXXth canon of Laodicea.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: It will be noted, as Zonaras remarks, that the monks must be counted among the laymen who are to be cut off, since they have no clerical character or tonsure.
